reasoning: Query is about cherry blossoms in Tohoku (not Tokyo). Tool fetches Akita spots broadly, and Kakunodate (sakura_meisho_100, famous for weeping cherries) appears in the truncated portion. However the top results are Lake Tazawa, Mt Chokai, fireworks — not sakura-focused. The cherry constraint is weakly handled. Tool only covers one of six Tohoku prefectures.
missing_data: Other Tohoku prefectures (Iwate, Miyagi, Yamagata, Fukushima, Aomori) cherry blossom spots missing. Iconic sites like Hirosaki Castle, Kitakami Tenshochi, Shiroishi Riverwalk absent. A multi-prefecture call or search_area with 'sakura tohoku' would be needed.
improvement_hint: Route query through intent classifier to recognize sakura + region combo, then call get_spots across multiple Tohoku prefectures filtering by sakura_meisho_100 kind, or use search_area({'q':'桜','region':'tohoku'}).

reasoning: get_local_specialty returns 24 items including major sake brands (越の三梅, 越乃寒梅/Koshi no Kanbai, 八海山/Hakkaisan, 久保田/Kubota) with Wikidata QIDs and descriptions. Intent is sake breweries; response covers brands well. Minor gap: MAFF GI entry kurosaki chamame (edamame) appears in the truncated portion — non-sake items mixed in, but tool is broad by design. Core brands are present and well-described.
missing_data: Physical brewery addresses and visit information (tour booking, hours) not available — these would require municipal scrape data. Shirataki/Jozen-Mizunogotoshi brands absent from visible results.
improvement_hint: Add a sake_brewery kind-filter option so agents can request only brewery-type items. Consider linking brewery Wikidata entities to their municipality scrape data for visitor info.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts WITH onsen in Hakuba area. Response includes Hakuba Happo-One ski resort (excellent) but then diverges to lake, art museum, botanical garden — none of these are ski+onsen combos. No onsen spots appear in the visible portion. The dual constraint (ski + onsen) is barely addressed; only ski is partially covered.
missing_data: Hakuba Cortina, Tsugaike, Goryu ski areas absent. Onsen facilities like Mimizuku-no-yu, Hakuba Happo-One Onsen, Mimizuku-no-yu entirely missing. The key combo constraint of ski+onsen colocation is not surfaced.
improvement_hint: Index onsen_resort and ski_resort kind combinations. For dual-constraint queries, augment with a second call using get_hotels or search for onsen kinds filtered by the same municipality to find colocated ski+onsen properties.

reasoning: Query is about autumn foliage (紅葉) in Kansai. Tool returns Kyoto spots broadly: Nijo Castle, To-ji, Sanzen-in (good autumn foliage), Ine-ura. Sanzen-in is excellent for autumn colors but is not tagged as such. Other Kansai prefectures (Osaka, Hyogo, Nara, Shiga) are entirely missing. The foliage constraint is not filtered — the response is generic Kyoto rather than foliage-specific Kansai.
missing_data: Arashiyama, Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji (top Kyoto koyo spots) absent from visible results. Nara Yoshino, Shiga Metasequoia-namiki, Hyogo Momijidani entirely missing. No koyo_meisho kind filtering applied.
improvement_hint: Add koyo_meisho or autumn_leaves kind tag to index. For region-level queries (Kansai = multiple prefectures), agent should call get_spots across Kyoto+Nara+Osaka+Shiga+Hyogo+Wakayama or use search_hybrid with koyo keyword.

reasoning: Query asks about famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai (Arabic). Tool fetches Nara spots. Mt Yoshino (sakura_meisho_100, top national cherry blossom site) is the first result — excellent match. Nara Park and Kofuku-ji also relevant. However coverage is Nara-only; other Kansai prefectures missing. Yoshino alone partially saves the score.
missing_data: Kyoto sakura spots (Maruyama, Heian Shrine, Kiyomizu), Osaka Mint garden, Himeji Castle cherry blossoms all absent. Query asked for Kansai broadly — Nara alone is incomplete but happens to include Japan's most iconic cherry site.
improvement_hint: For multi-prefecture region queries, extend tool calls to all Kansai prefectures, or use search_area with sakura + region filter. Returning only one prefecture for a regional query is a coverage gap.

reasoning: Query asks about remote Okinawa islands for stargazing. Tool returns general Okinawa spots: Nakagusuku Castle, Tamaudun, Shikina-en, Taketomi Island. Taketomi is an island hit (relevant), but none are tagged for stargazing. The 'remote islands' (離島) and 'stargazing' (星空) constraints are both poorly handled. No dark-sky or astronomy kind exists in the corpus.
missing_data: Iriomote, Yonaguni, Ishigaki (recognized stargazing spots), Miyako Island dark-sky areas entirely absent. No stargazing or dark_sky kind tags in the system to enable this type of query.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky or stargazing kind tag derived from municipal tourism content. Alternatively, tag remote island spots and let agents know the corpus lacks stargazing-specific data via a tool_scope_note.

reasoning: Query is about rural Hokkaido onsen lodging (Thai). get_hotels returns confirmed onsen_ryokan entries from Nihon Hito-yu association — these are genuinely rural/remote (Shiribeshi, Furano/Tokachidake, Asahidake). However coordinates are null for all hito_yu_kai entries, limiting practical usability. ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo also appears — urban hotel not matching 'rural' constraint.
missing_data: No coordinates for hito_yu_kai entries makes navigation impossible. Municipality data missing. Price range and booking URLs absent beyond the hitou.or.jp link.
improvement_hint: Enrich hito_yu_kai entries with geocoded coordinates from the postal address. Filter out urban hotel chains when the query specifies rural (田舎) context — or at least rank rural-tagged entries first.

reasoning: Query is about Seto Inland Sea islands (Vietnamese). Tool fetches Kagawa spots. Ritsurin Garden, Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle appear — these are mainland Kagawa, not islands. The island constraint is completely missed. No Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, Ogijima appear in visible results, though Kagawa does contain these.
missing_data: Naoshima (art island), Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima, Inujima — the key Setouchi islands — absent from visible results. Hiroshima prefecture's Ōkunoshima and Miyajima also relevant but not called.
improvement_hint: Add island kind filtering so agents can get_spots with kind='island' in Kagawa/Hiroshima/Okayama. Naoshima and Shodoshima should rank high given their tourism prominence and art museum content.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Tool returns 3 METI craft designations: 阿波正藍しじら織 (Awa indigo shijira weave) is directly relevant, 阿波和紙 mentions indigo-dyeing. However Otani Ware (ceramics) is off-topic for indigo. No workshop/experience venues are returned — only product designations. The 'workshop' experiential intent is missed.
missing_data: Actual workshop venues (藍染体験施設): Awa Ai-Indigo Museum, specific studio addresses absent. The query asks where to 'experience' not what the craft is — experiential venues not in the METI dataset.
improvement_hint: For experience/workshop queries, supplement get_local_specialty with a get_spots or search_area call targeting 藍染 workshop venues in Tokushima. Add experience_workshop kind tag to municipal scrape content.

reasoning: Query asks for Buddhist temple overnight stays (shukubo) on Mt Koya. get_hotels for Wakayama returns confirmed koyasan_shukubo entries: Annyoin, Ichijoin, Ekoin, Kitamuroin — exactly what was asked. All are confirmed, municipality=高野町, descriptions in Japanese, official association URLs. Phone numbers present. Excellent match to intent.
missing_data: No coordinates for shukubo entries (null). English descriptions absent — only Japanese. Pricing and availability info not present. English-speaking shukubo distinction not tagged.
improvement_hint: Geocode Koyasan shukubo entries; the area is small and precise coords can be sourced from OSM/Google. Add English translations for description_ja fields. Consider adding English-friendly or foreign-visitor-accessible tags.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area('武家屋敷') returns 146 matches with good nationwide spread: Matsue (Shimane), Fukui, Kakunodate (Akita), Chiran (Kagoshima), Aizu — all outside Kyoto. Descriptions mention heritage designations, buke_yashiki kinds, coordinates. The 'off-the-beaten-path' constraint is partially met — Kakunodate is popular but Chiran and Fukui entries are genuinely lesser-known.
missing_data: Kitsuki (Oita), Hagi (Yamaguchi) samurai districts not visible in truncated results but may be in the 146 results. Visitor hours and entry fees for each site would help an agent give practical guidance.
improvement_hint: Add prominence-based filtering option so agents can request lesser-known (low prominence_score) buke_yashiki results specifically. Current ranking by prominence score surfaces Kakunodate first, which is already well-known.

reasoning: Query asks for summer festivals in Akita (Indonesian). get_festivals returns 3 items — all bunka_intangible folk performing arts (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shrine), not summer festivals. The summer constraint is entirely missed. The famous Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり), one of Japan's top 3 summer festivals, is absent. national_heritage section includes UNESCO items but still no 'summer festival' date-filtered results.
missing_data: 秋田竿燈まつり (Aug 3-6), 大曲全国花火競技大会 (Aug, already in corpus as wikidata spot), 横手のぼんでん, 土崎神明社祭 (present but not contextualized as summer) — the summer date filter is not applied.
improvement_hint: get_festivals should support a month/season filter. Summer (June-August) filter would surface date-tagged events correctly. The Akita Kanto Festival should be indexed with a summer_festival or kanto_festival kind tag.

reasoning: Query asks for small fishing port towns in San'in region (Korean). Tool returns Shimane general spots: Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan, Mt Sanbe — none are fishing port towns. The 漁港/port town constraint is completely missed. Tottori is the other San'in prefecture but not queried. No fishing village or port kind appears in visible results.
missing_data: Kotoura, Sakaiminato (famous fishing port), Ōmori fishing village (near Iwami Ginzan), Hamada fishing town in Shimane absent. Tottori's Uradome coast and fishing ports also uncovered.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port or fishing_village kind tag derived from municipal tourism content. For San'in region queries, call both Shimane and Tottori prefectures. Consider adding Sakaiminato (水木しげるロード + fishing port) as a prominent entry.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about washi paper making experiences in Gifu. Tool returns 8 craft items: Mino Ware (ceramics), Hida Shunkei (lacquer), Nagiso Rokuro Zaiku (wood), Ichii Ittobori (wood carving) — no washi paper entry is visible in the truncated output. Mino Washi (美濃和紙), which is Gifu's most famous washi, should be present but is not in the first 4 items shown. The experience/workshop dimension is also not addressed.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi, METI craft 0905) not visible — likely in remaining 4 items but not surfaced first. Experience venues like Udatsu no Machinami washi workshop absent. The query's 'experience' intent requires venue data not in METI records.
improvement_hint: Rank washi-category items first when query contains 'washi' keyword. Add experience venue data from municipal scrapes to complement METI designation records. A search_area call with '美濃和紙 体験' would better address the experiential aspect.

reasoning: Query asks about fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula (Spanish). Tool fetches all Ishikawa spots: Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Bed and Craft — all in Kanazawa city area, not Noto Peninsula. Seven-story castle and major gardens are irrelevant. No Noto fishing villages (Wajima, Suzu, Noto town) appear. The geographic constraint (Noto Peninsula) is entirely missed by using prefecture-level query without municipality filter.
missing_data: Wajima (輪島), Suzu (珠洲), Noto-cho fishing hamlets, Kongo-ji coastline, Shiroyone Senmaida (千枚田 — terraced rice with sea view) all absent. Noto-specific municipality filter needed.
improvement_hint: For sub-prefectural geographic queries (Noto Peninsula = northern Ishikawa), add municipality filters for Noto-cho, Wajima-shi, Suzu-shi, Anamizu-cho rather than prefecture-level. The response as-is is entirely off-target geographically.

reasoning: Query asks for sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area('砂丘') returns 90 results with Tottori first (expected), then Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari Sand Dune Museum (Hokkaido), Fukiagehama (Kagoshima), Tsujido Beach (Kanagawa), and others. Good nationwide spread. The 'not just Tottori' constraint is addressed by the breadth of results. Ishikari and Fukiagehama are genuine lesser-known sand dune areas.
missing_data: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Hamamatsu), Sarobetsu (Hokkaido) sand plains not visible. Some results (Tsujido Beach, Kamiyama) are primarily beaches with minor sand dune classification — may confuse agents.
improvement_hint: Separate pure sand_dune kind from beach+sand_dune composite kind for ranking precision. Nakatajima (Shizuoka) and Sarobetsu should be added as sand dune entries if they appear in official source materials.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional crafts of San'in region (Chinese). get_local_specialty for Shimane returns 4 craft items with Chinese translations: Iwami-yaki ceramics, Sekishu Washi paper, Unshu abacus, Izumo stone lanterns. All are METI-designated, translations are high-confidence, descriptions well-translated. Covers both Iwami and Izumo sub-regions. Wikidata venues section adds Iwami Ginzan context.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture (the other San'in prefecture) crafts are absent — Inshū Washi (因州和紙), Yamamomo dye. The query says 山陰 (both Shimane + Tottori) but only Shimane queried.
improvement_hint: For 山陰 region queries, call both Shimane and Tottori get_local_specialty. Add a region parameter to tools so a single call covers multi-prefecture regions.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool fetches all Hokkaido spots broadly: Showa-Shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle, Daisetsuzan — none related to lavender. The lavender constraint is entirely missed. Furano's Farm Tomita is absent (though the user explicitly wanted alternatives to Furano, it should appear in the corpus). No lavender or flower farm kind exists.
missing_data: Lavender fields: Nakafurano, Kamifurano, Biei flower fields, Hokuryucho sunflower fields absent. No lavender or flower_farm kind tag in system. Even Furano Farm Tomita is absent from visible results.
improvement_hint: Add flower_field or lavender kind tag. Municipal scrapes of Furano, Biei, Nakafurano tourist sites should surface lavender venues. A search_area call with 'ラベンダー 北海道' would likely perform better for this intent.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional towns in Kyushu (Tagalog: 'Mga tradisyonal na bayan ng kape' = traditional coffee towns). The intent appears to be traditional old townscapes (古い町並み per topic). Tool fetches Oita spots: Usa Jingu shrine, Oka Castle, Kuju-cho tourism info, Kuju Mountains — none are traditional townscapes. The old town / historic district constraint is entirely missed. Mameda-machi appears in see_also but not main results.
missing_data: Hita Mameda-machi (historic merchant district in Oita), Usuki (stone Buddha town), Nakatsu castle town, Saeki historic area absent from main results. If query is about historic town districts, preservation_district kind should be surfaced.
improvement_hint: For historic townscape queries, filter by preservation_district or jokamachi kind. Hita Mameda-machi (Oita) and Usuki should rank highly. Add Kyushu region coverage by calling Saga and Nagasaki in addition to Oita.

reasoning: Query asks for kominka (old traditional house) stays in Hokuriku (Ishikawa). Tool returns Ishikawa hotels: two hito_yu_kai onsen_ryokan (白峰, 中宮温泉 — rural, possibly kominka-adjacent), then 'Student housing 1' (completely irrelevant) and AB Hotel (generic business hotel). No kominka_stay type entries visible. Bed and Craft (visible in L2-20 Ishikawa data) which does match kominka stays is absent here.
missing_data: Bed and Craft kominka hotel network (seen in Ishikawa data for L2-20) should appear. Student housing and generic business hotels are noise. Fukui and Toyama kominka stays (Hokuriku = 3 prefectures) entirely absent.
improvement_hint: Filter get_hotels by lodging_type kominka_stay or kominka. Exclude student_housing and generic hostel/business-hotel results from kominka queries. Cover all Hokuriku prefectures (Ishikawa + Toyama + Fukui) for regional queries.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage returns Wakayama results with Kongōbu-ji, Kumano Kodō, Kumano Sanzan — exactly the mountain religious sites of the Kii Peninsula. UNESCO WHS status confirmed. The tool_scope_note helpfully redirects to other tools for UNESCO-specific queries. Top canonical heritage items are highly relevant.
missing_data: Nachi Falls / Kumano Nachi Taisha individually, Yoshino-Omine range shrines from Nara side, Ise Jingu (Mie side of Kii Peninsula) absent — but these are other prefectures. Within Wakayama the coverage is strong.
improvement_hint: For Kii Peninsula queries, consider also calling Nara prefecture (Yoshino-Omine) and Mie (Kumano pilgrimage routes extend there). A region parameter covering the Kii Peninsula across three prefectures would be ideal.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks along Sea of Japan coast. Tool returns Niigata festivals (129 items). The 長岡まつり大花火大会 (Nagaoka Grand Fireworks, Aug 2-3) appears in the visible results — a direct hit, one of Japan's top 3 fireworks festivals. However the first two results are cherry blossom / mountain flower festivals (May), not fireworks. The summer/fireworks filter is not applied, requiring the agent to sift through 129 items. Sea of Japan coast coverage is Niigata-only.
missing_data: Omagari fireworks (Akita, August — already in wikidata spots), Niigata City fireworks, other Sea of Japan prefectures (Toyama, Ishikawa, Fukui, Tottori) fireworks absent. A hanabi filter or date filter would help enormously.
improvement_hint: Add event_subtype or kind filter for hanabi_taikai. The get_festivals tool should support month filtering so agents can request August events. Nagaoka is present but buried under spring flower events.

reasoning: Query asks for bamboo crafts in Oita. get_local_specialty returns 2 items: Hakata Ori (textile, multi-prefecture including Oita — somewhat peripheral) and 別府竹細工 (Beppu Bamboo Crafts) — a precise, direct match. Beppu Bamboo Crafts description is excellent with detailed techniques, materials (Madake bamboo), and production areas. see_also adds Mameda-machi and museums.
missing_data: Workshop/experience venues for bamboo craft in Beppu not provided — visitors would want to know where to see/do bamboo weaving. The Oita Prefectural Museum's bamboo collection absent.
improvement_hint: Link METI craft designations to venue data where available — Beppu bamboo craft shops and the Traditional Crafts Center (伝統工芸館) in Beppu should be linked from the craft entry.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool fetches Ehime spots. 大三島 (Ōmishima island) with cycling_route kind is an excellent match — it's part of the Shimanami Kaidō, Japan's most famous cycling route. However top results (Ozu Castle, Yuzuki Castle) are not cycling-relevant. Rural constraint partially met by Ōmishima. Coverage is Ehime-only; Kochi, Tokushima, Kagawa cycling areas absent.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaidō route itself as an entity, Kochi 四万十川 cycling, Yoshino River valley cycling (Tokushima), cycling_route kind filter not applied to prioritize relevant results.
improvement_hint: Add cycling_route kind filter to surface relevant spots first. Shimanami Kaidō should have its own top-level entry. For Shikoku cycling, call all 4 prefectures and filter by cycling_route kind.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo/Kyoto. get_traditional_arts (no filters) returns 183 items. Kabuki (UNESCO, Q199701) appears second. However the tool returns art forms (what), not venues (where). Bunraku, Chakkirako, and other unrelated arts dominate the visible results. The 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto' geographic constraint and the 'where to see' venue intent are both unaddressed.
missing_data: Specific regional kabuki venues: Konpira Grand Theatre (金丸座, Kotohira, Kagawa — oldest kabuki theatre), Misono-za (Nagoya), Hakataza (Fukuoka) entirely absent. The tool returns heritage designations, not performance venues.
improvement_hint: Add venue-type entries for regional kabuki theatres. For 'where to see X' queries, supplement get_traditional_arts with a get_spots or search_area call targeting 歌舞伎 + 劇場. Add kabuki_theater kind tag for venue discovery.
